cards.title: Belief geometry · GEO1
cards.direction: Belief geometry as the representational medium a theory is made of. The original move is a role assignment — as general relativity assigned manifolds to spacetime, GEO1 assigns the belief simplex the role of native epistemic state space: a theory is first a region, trajectory, obstruction, and repair policy in that geometry, and equations or causal graphs are charts exported only after it stabilizes.
cards.note: Original to GEO1 (the substrate layer): the role assignment of belief geometry as native medium, partial theories as regions, the forward causal pairing, and conservative extension. The finite anchors are proved; the full do-calculus lift is open. Figure from the private shape-of-uncertainty repo — confirm public use.

witnesses.claim: The centerpoint: the native object of theory formation is the geometry itself — belief state plus theory regions, allowed motions, diagnostics, and repair policies — and symbolic equations, causal graphs, and programmes are charts exported from it, not the thing being formed.
witnesses.svg: <rect x="16" y="62" width="92" height="44" fill="none" stroke="#16120b" stroke-width="1.2"/><text x="28" y="82" font-size="9">experimental</text><text x="28" y="95" font-size="9">records</text><path d="M110,84 L146,84" stroke="#16120b" stroke-width="1.1"/><path d="M140,80 L146,84 L140,88" fill="none" stroke="#16120b" stroke-width="1.1"/><rect x="148" y="56" width="106" height="56" fill="#3b4a93" fill-opacity="0.12" stroke="#3b4a93" stroke-width="1.4"/><text x="162" y="80" font-size="9.5" fill="#3b4a93">belief geometry</text><text x="162" y="95" font-size="8.5" fill="#3b4a93">points · faces · repairs</text><g stroke="#16120b" stroke-width="1.1" fill="none"><path d="M256,72 L300,40"/><path d="M256,84 L300,84"/><path d="M256,96 L300,128"/></g><rect x="302" y="26" width="120" height="30" fill="none" stroke="#16120b" stroke-width="1.1" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><text x="312" y="45" font-size="9" fill="#5f574a">equation chart</text><rect x="302" y="69" width="120" height="30" fill="none" stroke="#16120b" stroke-width="1.1" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><text x="312" y="88" font-size="9" fill="#5f574a">causal-graph chart</text><rect x="302" y="112" width="120" height="30" fill="none" stroke="#16120b" stroke-width="1.1" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><text x="312" y="131" font-size="9" fill="#5f574a">programme chart</text>

witnesses.claim: A partial theory is a region, not a sentence: a focal set is a set of mechanism profiles sharing the committed coordinates while leaving the rest open — ignorance carried as mass on a set, not forced into a probability over complete law names.
witnesses.svg: <rect x="18" y="40" width="46" height="34" fill="#16120b"/><text x="34" y="62" font-size="12" fill="#efe3c4">L</text><rect x="70" y="40" width="46" height="34" fill="#16120b"/><text x="86" y="62" font-size="12" fill="#efe3c4">D</text><rect x="122" y="40" width="46" height="34" fill="#16120b"/><text x="134" y="62" font-size="12" fill="#efe3c4">¬C</text><rect x="174" y="40" width="46" height="34" fill="#16120b"/><text x="190" y="62" font-size="12" fill="#efe3c4">N</text><rect x="226" y="40" width="46" height="34" fill="#16120b"/><text x="242" y="62" font-size="12" fill="#efe3c4">M</text><rect x="278" y="40" width="46" height="34" fill="#16120b"/><text x="290" y="62" font-size="12" fill="#efe3c4">¬F</text><rect x="330" y="40" width="46" height="34" fill="none" stroke="#b06a12" stroke-width="1.3" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><text x="344" y="62" font-size="12" fill="#b06a12">A?</text><rect x="382" y="40" width="46" height="34" fill="none" stroke="#b06a12" stroke-width="1.3" stroke-dasharray="4 3"/><text x="396" y="62" font-size="12" fill="#b06a12">B?</text><text x="18" y="98" font-size="9.5" fill="#16120b">committed where the evidence commits</text><text x="300" y="98" font-size="9" fill="#b06a12">open → repair experiments</text>

witnesses.claim: Forward causal pairing: a graph intervention induces a paired belief transport, and probability semantics is conserved exactly on the singleton-focal slice — the protected anchor. Lifting full graphical do-calculus beyond that slice is the open frontier.
witnesses.svg: <polygon points="60,140 230,24 400,140" fill="none" stroke="#16120b" stroke-width="1.4"/><line x1="118" y1="140" x2="342" y2="140" stroke="#2f6b4a" stroke-width="6" stroke-opacity="0.3"/><line x1="118" y1="140" x2="342" y2="140" stroke="#2f6b4a" stroke-width="1.4"/><circle cx="170" cy="140" r="3" fill="#2f6b4a"/><circle cx="230" cy="140" r="3" fill="#2f6b4a"/><circle cx="300" cy="140" r="3" fill="#2f6b4a"/><text x="138" y="156" font-size="10" fill="#2f6b4a">protected slice  =  probability semantics</text><text x="172" y="66" font-size="10" fill="#5f574a">G ⇝ G′  paired with belief transport</text><text x="150" y="100" font-size="11" fill="#2f6b4a">Bel = Pl = P  on singletons</text>

witnesses.claim: The medium grows only conservatively: a vacuous frame refinement preserves old belief and plausibility and commutes with combination, but it also preserves hard conflict — so adding hidden coordinates demands explicit conservation assumptions, modelled by a forced representation cover.
witnesses.svg: <rect x="40" y="50" width="120" height="56" fill="none" stroke="#16120b" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="58" y="44" font-size="9.5" fill="#5f574a">frame Θ</text><text x="66" y="83" font-size="11">m,  Bel(E)</text><path d="M168,78 L232,78" stroke="#16120b" stroke-width="1.2"/><path d="M225,73 L232,78 L225,83" fill="none" stroke="#16120b" stroke-width="1.2"/><text x="180" y="70" font-size="10" fill="#3b4a93">ρ↑</text><rect x="240" y="50" width="160" height="56" fill="none" stroke="#16120b" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="258" y="44" font-size="9.5" fill="#5f574a">refined Θ′</text><text x="258" y="83" font-size="11" fill="#2f6b4a">Bel(ρ⁻¹E) preserved</text><text x="60" y="130" font-size="9.5" fill="#b06a12">A ∩ B = ∅  ⟹  ρ⁻¹A ∩ ρ⁻¹B = ∅  (conflict survives)</text>

cards.author: Dhruv Gupta

cards.title: Belief geometry · GEO1
cards.direction: What the scientist computes on a belief state: belief and plausibility as readouts, identifiability as the complement of a collision set, residual dependence after fixing the shared variables, and the capacity regularity that lets continuous records be measured at all. Diagnostics native to the geometry, not bolted on after a model is chosen.
cards.note: Original to GEO1 (the diagnostics layer): the backward collision reading, the residual-dependence test, and the continuous capacity bridge. Status (Appendix C): the Choquet branch is conditional on Polish / analytic / capacity hypotheses; an automatic record→evidence rule remains open.

witnesses.claim: Belief and plausibility are the readouts of the state, and ordinary probability is exactly the singleton-focal readout — the credal interval [Bel, Pl] is what a single distribution throws away.
witnesses.svg: <line x1="60" y1="64" x2="380" y2="64" stroke="#16120b" stroke-width="1.2"/><line x1="140" y1="55" x2="140" y2="73" stroke="#2f6b4a" stroke-width="1.8"/><line x1="300" y1="55" x2="300" y2="73" stroke="#2f6b4a" stroke-width="1.8"/><path d="M140,64 L300,64" stroke="#2f6b4a" stroke-width="3" stroke-opacity="0.25"/><circle cx="222" cy="64" r="4.5" fill="#3b4a93"/><text x="120" y="46" font-size="10" fill="#2f6b4a">Bel(E)</text><text x="284" y="46" font-size="10" fill="#2f6b4a">Pl(E)</text><text x="210" y="92" font-size="10" fill="#3b4a93">P(E)</text><text x="64" y="110" font-size="9.5" fill="#5f574a">probability = the singleton-focal readout; the credal interval is wider</text>

witnesses.claim: Backward direction: a belief displacement is read diagnostically to infer which manipulation occurred, and non-identifiability becomes a geometric object — an affine collision set whose complement is identifiability and whose structure scores the next experiment.
witnesses.svg: <circle cx="70" cy="80" r="4" fill="#16120b"/><text x="52" y="76" font-size="11">m</text><path d="M78,76 L210,40" stroke="#16120b" stroke-width="1.2"/><path d="M202,38 L210,40 L204,46" fill="none" stroke="#16120b" stroke-width="1.2"/><text x="216" y="42" font-size="10" fill="#5f574a">ρ(T₁m)</text><path d="M78,82 L210,76" stroke="#16120b" stroke-width="1.2"/><path d="M202,72 L210,76 L203,81" fill="none" stroke="#16120b" stroke-width="1.2"/><text x="216" y="80" font-size="10" fill="#5f574a">ρ(T₂m)</text><path d="M78,86 L210,122" stroke="#b06a12" stroke-width="1.4"/><circle cx="214" cy="122" r="5" fill="none" stroke="#b06a12" stroke-width="1.4"/><text x="226" y="126" font-size="10" fill="#b06a12">ρ(T₃m) = ρ(T₄m)</text><text x="250" y="146" font-size="9.5" fill="#b06a12">collision  =  non-identifiable</text>

witnesses.claim: A diagnostic the geometry computes on itself: after the shared variables are fixed, the private coordinates are still dependent iff the fiber is not a product — so a candidate explanation can be checked for whether its shared variables actually explain the dependence.
witnesses.svg: <g stroke="#16120b" stroke-width="1"><rect x="44" y="36" width="86" height="86" fill="none"/><line x1="87" y1="36" x2="87" y2="122"/><line x1="44" y1="79" x2="130" y2="79"/></g><circle cx="65" cy="58" r="4" fill="#16120b"/><circle cx="109" cy="58" r="4" fill="#16120b"/><circle cx="65" cy="100" r="4" fill="#16120b"/><circle cx="109" cy="100" r="4" fill="#16120b"/><text x="48" y="138" font-size="10" fill="#2f6b4a">product fiber:  I = 0  (explained)</text><g stroke="#16120b" stroke-width="1"><rect x="262" y="36" width="86" height="86" fill="none"/><line x1="305" y1="36" x2="305" y2="122"/><line x1="262" y1="79" x2="348" y2="79"/></g><circle cx="283" cy="58" r="4" fill="#b06a12"/><circle cx="327" cy="100" r="4" fill="#b06a12"/><text x="258" y="138" font-size="10" fill="#b06a12">diagonal:  I = 1 bit  (residual)</text>

witnesses.code:
theorem mutualInformation_eq_zero_iff_product :
    I(P₁ ; P₂ | S = s) = 0  ↔  F_s = π₁ F_s × π₂ F_s

witnesses.claim: The continuous witness bridge: nonsingleton belief mass requires a set-valued observation, and a continuous, existential witness event is measured through Choquet capacity — analytic events are capacitable from inside by compact sets and null-measurable under finite measures.
witnesses.svg: <rect x="28" y="34" width="150" height="58" fill="none" stroke="#16120b" stroke-width="1.3"/><text x="38" y="58" font-size="10">set-valued  Y : Ω → 2^Θ\{∅}</text><text x="38" y="78" font-size="10" fill="#3b4a93">m(A) = P(Y = A)</text><path d="M86,96 L86,118" stroke="#16120b" stroke-width="1.1"/><path d="M82,112 L86,118 L90,112" fill="none" stroke="#16120b" stroke-width="1.1"/><text x="40" y="136" font-size="9" fill="#5f574a">finite branch → belief mass</text><path d="M230,118 C240,52 360,46 392,70 C360,128 250,132 230,118 Z" fill="none" stroke="#16120b" stroke-width="1.3"/><rect x="262" y="82" width="34" height="24" fill="#2f6b4a" fill-opacity="0.18" stroke="#2f6b4a" stroke-width="1.1"/><rect x="306" y="86" width="28" height="20" fill="#2f6b4a" fill-opacity="0.18" stroke="#2f6b4a" stroke-width="1.1"/><text x="312" y="62" font-size="9.5" fill="#5f574a">analytic A ⊆ X (Polish)</text><text x="232" y="140" font-size="9" fill="#2f6b4a">c(A) = sup { c(K) : K ⊆ A compact }</text>

witnesses.code:
theorem measure_isChoquetCapacity :
    a finite Borel measure on a Polish space is a Choquet capacity
theorem AnalyticSet.nullMeasurableSet :
    A analytic  ⟹  NullMeasurableSet A μ   (μ finite)

theorem learn_measurable : a jointly-measurable learned rule
    stays measurable in future records once training is fixed
theorem attention_produces_non_borel_bad_event :
    a natural attention construction yields a non-Borel bad set
    -- Borel-only handling would overclaim; analytic layer needed

cards.author: Dhruv Gupta
